muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server --use-datastar
muxt check

# Default signal marshaling uses the standard library encoding/json, never the
# go-json-experiment backport (jsonv2 is opt-in via --json-v2).
grep '"encoding/json"' template_routes.go
! grep 'go-json-experiment' template_routes.go

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "POST /count Increment(ctx, signal)" -}}{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.25
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

type Count struct {
	Count int `json:"count"`
}

// Increment responds with a datastar-patch-signals JSON body. The signal
// callback marshals the result; the second argument is onlyIfMissing, which has
// no representation in a plain application/json body and is ignored.
func (Server) Increment(ctx context.Context, signal func(data Count, onlyIfMissing bool) error) {
	_ = signal(Count{Count: 5}, false)
}
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestIncrement(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/count", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
		t.Fatalf("status = %d, want %d", rec.Code, http.StatusOK)
	}
	if ct := rec.Header().Get("Content-Type"); ct != "application/json" {
		t.Fatalf("content-type = %q, want application/json", ct)
	}
	if got := strings.TrimSpace(rec.Body.String()); got != `{"count":5}` {
		t.Fatalf("body = %q, want %q", got, `{"count":5}`)
	}
}
